name: Immune-Mediated Haemolytic Anaemia (IMHA)
description: IMHA is a life-threatening condition in which the immune system destroys the dog's own red blood cells. It can be primary (idiopathic) or secondary to triggers such as drugs, infections, or neoplasia. Mortality rates can reach 40-60% in severe cases.
treatment_overview: Immunosuppression with high-dose prednisolone is the mainstay; azathioprine, cyclosporine, or mycophenolate are added for refractory cases. Blood transfusions provide life support. Anticoagulants reduce thromboembolism risk.
prevention_tips: Avoid unnecessary vaccinations in previously affected dogs
Avoid triggering drugs in known cases
